title: Stop the Invasion of Women’s Spaces Act
short_title: Stop the Invasion of Women’s Spaces Act
plain_summary: This bill would stop the federal government from giving money to any group or organization that lets people use bathrooms, locker rooms, or changing rooms that don't match their biological sex. This includes private companies and state and local governments. The rule doesn't apply when emergency medical workers or police officers need to enter these spaces while doing their jobs. If an organization breaks this rule, they would lose their federal funding.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Stop the Invasion of Women's Spaces Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Prohibition
sections.text: The federal government cannot give money to any organization that lets people use bathrooms, locker rooms, or changing rooms that are meant for the opposite biological sex. Biological sex means whether someone is male or female based on their body. An organization includes private companies, government agencies that are not federal, and state, tribal, or local governments. There are two exceptions to this rule. First, emergency medical workers can enter these spaces when responding to a medical emergency. Second, police officers can enter these spaces when chasing a suspect or during an active investigation. For this law, female means a person who has or would normally have a reproductive system that makes eggs. Male means a person who has or would normally have a reproductive system that makes sperm. Property means any building, land, or real estate that an organization owns, rents, controls, or uses. Single-sex facility means a space meant for one biological sex only, including restrooms, locker rooms, and changing rooms.
